meta.description: Single-annotator blind gold labels for the AG2 dogfood traces in raw_ag2_records.json. See gold_labels.md for rationale and evidence per trace.
meta.known_limitation: Single annotator, unlike MAD's 3-annotator ground truth. A second qualified annotator on the PRESENT traces is the honest next step before citing this as validated ground truth (design doc Approach C).
meta.revision: Updated post-judge-run per evals/dogfood/adjudication.md: FM-3.2 added to rate-limiter and pubsub-broker after adjudicating the judge's FM-3.2 disagreement against trace evidence (2 of 3 judge FM-3.2 firings upheld as real, missed findings; linked-list-merge's rejected). Pass-1 labels below are otherwise unchanged.
meta.trace_id_note: Keys here are the raw dogfood task id (matches TASKS[].id in run_ag2.py). adapters/mast.py::to_trace() will prefix these as 'mad-<id>' when parsing raw_ag2_records.json -- that's a cosmetic artifact of reusing the MAD adapter unchanged, not a claim these are MAD records.

linked-list-merge.notes: Clean. Verified independently: node-reuse and all 6 traced test cases correct.

rate-limiter.notes: Planner (turn 3) writes Coder's full implementation and Tester's full test suite plus a self-claimed verification pass, all in one turn, contradicting its own system message. Content itself verified correct independently. FM-1.3 considered for the redundant re-delivery/re-verification by the real Coder/Tester but treated as a consequence of FM-1.2, not double-fired -- see gold_labels.md. FM-1.2 independently confirmed by a second, fresh-context annotator pass (adjudication.md). FM-3.2 added post-adjudication (adjudication.md) but now DISPUTED: the same second annotator independently rejected it, reading Tester's acknowledged timing risk as legitimate judgment rather than an unexamined gap. Left present pending real resolution -- see adjudication.md's 'Second annotator pass' section.

cache-refactor.notes: Clean. Planner delegates via spec only, waits for real responses. Fix verified correct independently against the docstring.

csv-report-cli.notes: Clean. FM-2.2 explicitly considered and rejected: the task sanctioned 'figure out what's reasonable' as an alternative to asking, and Planner did so with documented, harmless assumptions.

pubsub-broker.notes: Clearest FM-1.2 instance in the batch. Turn 5's literal name field is 'Planner' but content is headed '## Tester -- Verification Report' -- a direct name/content mismatch, not just stylistic overreach. Planner also ghost-writes Coder's implementation and bugfix. Technical content (the BaseException fix) is correct; pure role-boundary violation. FM-1.2 independently confirmed by a second, fresh-context annotator pass, which found this the most severe of the three PRESENT traces (adjudication.md). FM-3.2 added post-adjudication (adjudication.md) but now DISPUTED: the second annotator called the adversarial suite genuinely thorough and did not independently surface the same-topic-reentrant-publish gap this file's adjudication is built on. Left present pending real resolution -- see adjudication.md's 'Second annotator pass' section.

state-machine-todo.notes: Clean, thorough. Tester verifies the given test plus two additional un-requested cycles.

perf-optimization.notes: Planner (turn 2) ghost-writes Coder's full O(n log n) algorithm and starts Tester's verification; real Coder never gets an independent turn anywhere in the trace. Algorithm and complexity verified correct independently. FM-1.2 independently confirmed by a second, fresh-context annotator pass, which called this the structurally cleanest of the three cases (adjudication.md).

raise-vs-none-dispute.notes: Borderline but called clean: Planner resolves the stated disagreement unilaterally and hands Coder literal code, but Coder and Tester both still contribute genuine independent work afterward (unlike the three PRESENT traces, where Coder/Tester's contribution is fully subsumed by Planner). Flagged in gold_labels.md as the one call most likely to flip under a second annotator.
